title: BeyondTrust Privileged Sessions Started Outside Business Hours
dataSources: `sourcetype=beyondtrust:session` (or normalized PAM session events forwarded from BeyondTrust)
app: BeyondTrust TA / Splunk CIM Email & Authentication patterns (org deployment)
spl:
index=pam sourcetype="beyondtrust:session" earliest=-7d
| eval user=coalesce(user, UserName, admin_user, "")
| eval hour=strftime(_time, "%H")
| eval dow=strftime(_time, "%u")
| where (hour<7 OR hour>=19) OR dow IN ("6","7")
| stats count values(target_host) as targets by user
| sort -count
description: Privileged PAM sessions that begin late night or on weekends warrant review because they align with ransomware operators and rogue administrators evading oversight.
value: Operationalizes a simple time-based control that works even when session recordings are retained elsewhere, giving SOC analysts a prioritized review queue.
implementation: Adjust business-hour boundaries and weekend definition for your locale. Map `target_host` field names to your parser. Exclude maintenance accounts via lookup. Pair with on-call schedules to reduce false positives.
visualization: Table (user × targets), timeline of off-hours sessions, pie (weekday vs weekend).

references.title: BeyondTrust Password Safe / Cloud Dashboard for Splunk
Splunk Lantern — privileged access monitoring patterns

dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable: this use case is not built on a CIM data model. Rely on the search in Step 2 against your raw directory, IdP, PAM, or device feeds.
knownFalsePositives: Global teams, weekend cutovers, vendor support windows, and on-call maintenance that is normal in your org but off local business hours — tune time zones and account exclude lists.
grandmaExplanation: We flag when someone starts a privileged vault session at odd hours so you can review before off-hours access turns into unnoticed abuse.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure: BeyondTrust TA / Splunk CIM Email & Authentication patterns (org deployment).
• Data sources: `sourcetype=beyondtrust:session` (or normalized PAM session events forwarded from BeyondTrust).

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Adjust business-hour boundaries and weekend definition for your locale. Map `target_host` field names to your parser. Exclude maintenance accounts via lookup. Pair with on-call schedules to reduce false positives.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert

```spl
index=pam sourcetype="beyondtrust:session" earliest=-7d
| eval user=coalesce(user, UserName, admin_user, "")
| eval hour=strftime(_time, "%H")
| eval dow=strftime(_time, "%u")
| where (hour<7 OR hour>=19) OR dow IN ("6","7")
| stats count values(target_host) as targets by user
| sort -count
```

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with BeyondTrust session history in the vendor console for the same users, targets, and timestamps.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add to a dashboard or alert; document the owner. Table (user × targets), timeline of off-hours sessions, pie (weekday vs weekend).
